questions.question: In the Feature Branch Workflow, what is the primary rule about the 'main' branch?
options: It is only used for hotfixes
It should never be merged into

questions.question: How does code get into 'main' in the Feature Branch Workflow?
What should happen to feature branches after they are merged into main?
options: They should be renamed with a 'merged/' prefix
They should be kept for future reference
They should be archived to a separate remote

questions.question: Which command creates a new branch and switches to it in one step?
options: git branch feature/new && git checkout feature/new

questions.question: In Gitflow, which branch serves as the integration branch for features?
In Gitflow, what is the purpose of a 'hotfix/*' branch?
Which branch in Gitflow holds production releases only?
In the Forking Workflow, what does 'upstream' refer to?
options: Your local copy of the repository
The original repository you forked from
questions.answer: The original repository you forked from

questions.question: What does 'origin' refer to in a forking workflow after you clone your fork?
Which command adds the original repository as a remote named 'upstream'?
To sync your fork with the latest changes from the original repository, which two commands do you run in order?
options: git pull origin main, then git push upstream main
git fetch upstream, then git merge upstream/main
git clone upstream, then git push origin main
git rebase main, then git fetch upstream
questions.answer: git fetch upstream, then git merge upstream/main

questions.question: Which branch naming prefix is used for new functionality?
Which branch naming prefix is used for maintenance tasks?
Which command deletes a local branch that has already been merged?
What does 'git log main..HEAD' show?
options: Commits on your current branch that are not on main
The difference between the first and last commit
Only merge commits between main and HEAD
questions.answer: Commits on your current branch that are not on main

questions.question: What does 'git diff main...HEAD --stat' display?
options: A full patch of every changed line
A summary of all changes compared to main
Only the names of deleted files
The commit messages between main and HEAD
questions.answer: A summary of all changes compared to main

questions.question: Which collaboration workflow is most commonly used by open source projects?
In the Feature Branch Workflow, feature branches are intended to be:
Which command lists all local branches?
After pushing your synced fork to GitHub, which command do you use?
Which command shows all configured remotes with their URLs?
In Gitflow, what is the purpose of a 'release/*' branch?
options: To prepare a release for production
To fix critical bugs in production
questions.answer: To prepare a release for production

questions.question: Which of the following is a best practice when making commits on a feature branch?
options: Make one large commit with all changes
Commit only when the feature is completely done
Avoid commit messages to save time

questions.question: You can delete multiple branches in a single command using:
What is the first step in the Forking Workflow before you can clone and work locally?
options: Create a new branch on the original repository
Run git init in a local directory
